verdict.answer_first: A states the command immediately: "Use `find`..." followed by `find /path/to/dir -type f -exec stat -f '%z %N' {} + | sort -rn | head -10`; B first frames options before the command.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to the primary solution with "Use `find`...", while B says "Here are a few good options" and surveys several variants.
verdict.fabrication: B appears to add non-portable macOS advice: "Add ... `-maxdepth 1` to the `find`"; `-maxdepth` is not standard BSD/macOS find. A: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it gives the exact macOS command first and keeps caveats short; B adds extra variants including a questionable one.
